Overview

Address
900 LONDON BOULEVARD, PORTSMOUTH, VA 23704

Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.08 3.73 3.18 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.22 0.65 0.22 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.94 0.83 0.97 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.92 2.25 1.99 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.15 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.72
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.12
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.82
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
80.4%
annual
RN turnover
100.0%
annual
Administrators departed
3
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.32
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.96
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2021-05-28 20 13 7 203 1 203
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2019-02-07 25 14 11 332 2 498
Total weighted health score
276.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (64)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
56 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
3 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-09-19 2025-12-08
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-09-19 2025-12-08
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-09-19 2025-12-08
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-09-19 2025-12-08
0678 Provide basic life support, including CPR, prior to the arrival of emergency medical personnel , subject to physician orders and the resident’s advance directives. J 2025-09-19 2025-12-09
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-09-19 2026-01-21
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-09-19 2025-12-08
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. G 2024-10-02 2024-09-19
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-05-23 2024-06-19
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2024-05-23 2024-06-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-05-23 2024-06-19
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2024-05-23 2024-06-19
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-03-22 2024-06-19
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2024-03-22 2024-06-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-03-22 2024-06-19
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. K 2024-03-22 2024-04-23
0741 Ensure that the facility has sufficient staff members who possess the competencies and skills to meet the behavioral health needs of residents. K 2024-03-22 2024-04-23
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2024-03-22 2024-06-19
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. E 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. E 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. F 2021-05-28 2021-07-05
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2019-02-07 2019-04-17
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. D 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly D 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. C 2019-02-07 2019-03-09
0156 Give residents a notice of rights, rules, services and charges. E 2017-07-13 2017-08-13
0159 Properly hold, secure, and manage each resident's personal money which is deposited with the nursing home. E 2017-07-13 2017-08-13
0167 Allow residents to easily view the results of the nursing home's most recent inspection. C 2017-07-13 2017-08-13
0241 Provide care for residents in a way that maintains or improves their dignity and respect in full recognition of their individuality. D 2017-07-13 2017-08-13
0276 Assure that each resident’s assessment is updated at least once every 3 months. D 2017-07-13 2017-08-13

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
